name: Electronics and Instrumentation Engineering
description: A specialized program focused on measurement systems, sensors, industrial automation, and control instrumentation. Prepares students for careers in process industries, automation, and biomedical instrumentation.

subjects.name: Sensors and Transducers
subjects.description: Operating principles, characteristics, and applications of sensors and transducers for measuring temperature, pressure, flow, level, displacement, and other physical quantities. Covers signal conditioning and smart sensors.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate sensors for given measurement applications
Design signal conditioning circuits for sensor outputs
Calibrate sensors and analyze measurement uncertainty
Interface smart sensors with microcontrollers and data acquisition systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for all process industries, automotive sensor systems, IoT devices, and medical diagnostics.

subjects.name: Industrial Instrumentation
subjects.description: Measurement techniques for industrial parameters including pressure, temperature, flow, level, and density. Covers industrial transmitters, smart instrumentation, and fieldbus communication protocols.
learning_outcomes: Design measurement loops for key process variables
Configure smart transmitters using HART and fieldbus protocols
Calibrate industrial instruments according to standards
Troubleshoot common instrumentation issues in industrial plants
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for oil and gas, chemical, pharmaceutical, and food processing industries.

subjects.name: Process Control
subjects.description: Principles of automatic process control including block diagrams, dynamic behavior of processes, tuning of PID controllers, cascade, feedforward, and ratio control strategies. Covers multivariable control and optimization.
learning_outcomes: Model dynamic behavior of industrial processes
Tune PID controllers using Ziegler-Nichols and other methods
Design cascade, feedforward, and ratio control systems
Implement advanced control strategies for multivariable processes
subjects.industry_relevance: Core skill for chemical plants, refineries, power plants, and pharmaceutical manufacturing.

subjects.name: PLC and SCADA Systems
subjects.description: Design and programming of Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs), ladder logic, function block diagrams, and SCADA systems for industrial automation. Covers HMI design, communication protocols, and DCS integration.
learning_outcomes: Program PLCs using ladder logic and structured text
Design SCADA screens and configure alarm management
Implement communication between PLC, SCADA, and field devices
Develop automated control solutions for industrial processes
subjects.industry_relevance: Widely used in manufacturing, water treatment, power plants, and oil and gas facilities worldwide.

subjects.name: Embedded Systems for Instrumentation
subjects.description: Design of embedded systems for instrumentation applications including microcontroller selection, sensor interfacing, real-time data acquisition, and communication protocols. Covers ARM Cortex-M and IoT integration.
learning_outcomes: Design embedded data acquisition systems for sensors
Implement real-time firmware for measurement applications
Interface embedded systems with industrial communication protocols
Develop IoT-enabled instrumentation solutions
subjects.industry_relevance: Driving smart instrumentation, Industry 4.0, and wireless sensor networks in industrial IoT.

subjects.name: Biomedical Instrumentation
subjects.description: Medical instrumentation systems including ECG, EEG, EMG, blood pressure monitors, pacemakers, and medical imaging devices. Covers patient safety, biosignal processing, and regulatory standards.
learning_outcomes: Design biopotential amplifier circuits for ECG and EEG acquisition
Analyze and filter biomedical signals for diagnostic information
Evaluate medical device safety standards and electrical safety
Interpret medical imaging principles including X-ray, MRI, and ultrasound
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for the medical device industry including patient monitoring, diagnostic equipment, and therapeutic devices.

subjects.name: Measurement Systems
subjects.description: Fundamentals of measurement science including static and dynamic characteristics, uncertainty analysis, statistical quality control, and design of measurement systems. Covers ADCs, DACs, and data acquisition hardware.
learning_outcomes: Analyze static and dynamic characteristics of measurement systems
Perform uncertainty analysis and error propagation calculations
Design data acquisition systems with proper sampling and anti-aliasing
Apply statistical methods for quality control in measurement
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for quality assurance, metrology, testing laboratories, and calibration services.

subjects.name: Analytical Instrumentation
subjects.description: Instrumentation for chemical and physical analysis including spectroscopy (UV-Vis, IR, NMR), chromatography (GC, HPLC), mass spectrometry, and electrochemical analysis methods.
learning_outcomes: Explain operating principles of spectroscopic instruments
Analyze samples using chromatographic techniques
Interpret analytical data from mass spectrometry
Select appropriate analytical instruments for specific applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for pharmaceutical QC, environmental monitoring, food safety testing, and forensic laboratories.

subjects.name: Control System Components
subjects.description: Study of control system components including actuators, valves, positioners, servomotors, stepper motors, and hydraulic/pneumatic control elements. Covers transfer function modeling and system integration.
learning_outcomes: Select and size control valves for process applications
Model and analyze servomotor and stepper motor systems
Design electro-pneumatic and electro-hydraulic control systems
Integrate control components into complete automation solutions
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for valve manufacturing, motion control, robotics, and heavy machinery automation.
